title: Brachychiton garrawayae
taxonomy: Brachychiton garrawayae is a species of flowering plant in the family Sterculiaceae. This plant is native to Australia and is a member of the genus Brachychiton, which is commonly known as the bottle tree or kurrajong. The Sterculiaceae family is a group of flowering plants that also includes the cacao tree and the kapok tree. The classification of Brachychiton garrawayae places it within the order Malvales and the clade Angiosperms. Further classification of this plant also includes the rank of subclass Rosidae within the clade of eudicots.

intro: Brachychiton garrawayae, also known as the Queensland bottle tree, is a large, deciduous tree native to Australia. The Queensland bottle tree has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, antimalarial properties, and wound healing due to its purported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Bioactive constituents of Brachychiton garrawayae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Brachychiton garrawayae include teas, extracts, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms of malaria.
